query: I have per-row log-likelihoods from both my base model and my SFT fine-tune on a 2000-row held-out eval. I want to know whether the fine-tune is better. I ran a Shapiro-Wilk on the per-row differences (LL_finetune - LL_base) and got p = 0.0008. Now what?
expected_behavior: Identifies that Shapiro p = 0.0008 rejects Normality of the per-row differences and that paired-t therefore violates its parametric assumption — recommends Wilcoxon signed-rank instead, with the Shapiro result named as the gating assumption check that drove the choice
Recommends a paired non-parametric effect size with a confidence interval (paired rank-biserial r or paired Cliff's delta with bootstrap 95% CI), NOT just the Wilcoxon p-value

Names the direction of the effect explicitly (e.g., 'fine-tune log-likelihood is higher than base' or 'positive median of the per-row differences'), not just 'the test was significant' or 'they differ'
